SUPPLEMENT TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ARORA, VASU D.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VASU ARORA'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE ORDER OF 9/29/14, BEING A FRAUD ON THE COURT BY THE DEFENDANTS WHO THEREBY LACKED JURISIDCTION; AND BECAUSE OF MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE AND EXCUSABLE NEGLECT UNDER CCP 473(B) IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY UNDER CCP 1005(B). THE MOTION WAS FILED AND SERVED ON AUGUST 4. MOVING PARTY ONLY PROVIDED 14 COURT DAYS NOTICE AND DID NOT ACCOUNT FOR THE FIVE ADDITIONAL CALENDAR DAYS REQUIRED FOR MAILING. ADDITIONALLY, THE MOTION IS DENIED ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION AS TO WHY HE DID NOT FILE AN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO DISMISS OR ATTEND THE JULY 29, 2014 HEARING. THE PROOFS OF SERVICE FILED BY DEFENDANTS ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF WAS NOTIFIED ABOUT THE JULY 29, 2014 HEARING DATE VIA EMAIL AND MAIL. (MARK R. MEYER DECLARATION, EX. A.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION OF THE PLAINTIF'S MEMORANDUM OF POINTS IN ITS SUPPORT TO MODIFY, AMEND OR REVOKE THE ORDER OF AUGUST 22, 2014, UNDER CCP 473; MISTAKE INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, EXCUSABLE NEGLECT, AND TO HOLD THE HEARING ON BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BY THE DEFENDANTS FILED BY PLAINTIFF ARORA, VASU D. HEARING SET FOR OCT-15-2014 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BASU D. ARORA'S MOTION TO AMEND OR REVOKE THE ORDER OF AUGUST 22, 2014, UNDER CCP 473; MISTAKE INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, EXCUSABLE NEGLECT, AND TO HOLD THE HEARING ON BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS OFF CALENDAR. THE PROOF OF SERVICE STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS SERVED BY EMAIL. RULE OF COURT 2.251(B)(1)(A) REQUIRES A SHOWING OF CONSENT TO ACCEPT ELECTRONIC SERVICE. NO SHOWING HAS BEEN MADE THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE AGREED TO ELECTRONIC SERVICE. NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR# 5976.(302/MJM)

MOTION TO DECLARE VASU A. ARORA A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY DEFENDANT ARNOLD LAUB, PC D/B/A LAW OFFICES OF ARNOLD LAUB (INCORRECTLY SUED AS ARNOLD LAUB AND LAW OFFICE OF ARNOLD LAUB) HEARING SET FOR JAN-22-2015 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, MTN TO REQUESTING RECONSIDERATION TO MODIFY, AMEND OR REVOKE PREVIOUS ORDER,IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY UNDER CCP 1008 AND THE COURT LACKS THE JURISDICTION TO ENTERTAIN THE MOTION BECAUSE JUDGMENT WAS ENTERED ON JULY 29, 2014. SEE RAMON V. AEROSPACE CORP. (1996) 50 CAL.APP.4TH 1233, 1236. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/AJR)

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO DECLARE VASU A. ARORA A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT IS GRANTED UNDER CCP 391(B)(2). THE COURT FINDS THAT MR. ARORA QUALIFIES AS A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT UNDER THAT SECTION. HOWEVER, THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THE REQUIREMENTS HAVE BEEN MET UNDER CCP 391(B)(3). MR. ARORA SHALL BE PROHIBITED FROM FILING ANY LITIGATION AGAINST ARNOLD LAUB DBA LAW OFFICE OF ARNOLD LAUB OR THE ATTORNEYS OF THAT OFFICE WITHOUT FIRST OBTAINING LEAVE OF THE PRESIDING JUDGE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/AJR)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICES OF ARNOLD LAUB AND 807 MONTGOMERY STREET ASSOCIATES LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. (CCP SEC. 430.10(E) AND (F).) THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED IN SF SUPERIOR COURT CASE CGC-10-501595. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS INVOLVE THE SAME PRIMARY RIGHT, INADEQUATE PAYMENT OF WAGES AND INSUFFICIENT MEAL AND REST BREAKS, RAISED IN PREVIOUS LITIGATION AGAINST DEFENDANT (SF SUPERIOR COURT CASE CGC-10-501595), AND THEREFORE ARE BARRED BY RES JUDICATA. ALTERNATIVELY, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT ENTERED INTO ON JANUARY 6, 2012, IN WHICH PLAINTIFF AGREED TO RELEASE DEFENDANT FROM "ANY CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF EMPLOYEE'S EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP WITH THE COMPANY." (FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC"), EX. 5, SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, PAR. 11.) AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLEARLY ALLEGE, IF PLAINTIFF CAN IN GOOD FAITH, HOW DEFENDANT BREACHED THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-11-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICES OF ARNOLD LAUB AND 807 MONTGOMERY STREET ASSOCIATES LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 11, 2014 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 11, 2014 AT 4:00 PM, OR THE DEMURRER WILL BE SUSTAINED AND MOTION TO STRIKE WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Mtn To Requesting Reconsideration To Modify, Amend Or Revoke Previous Order
Off Calendar Jan-22-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff VASU D. ARORA, Tel No. (408) 365-1277 appearing in pro per;
Atty. MARK R. MEYER of Losch & Ehrlich, Tel No. (415) 956-8400 appearing telephonically for Defendants Arnold Laub, P.C. dba Law Offices of Arnold Laub.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF VASU D. ARORA'S Motion Requesting Reconsideration To Modify, Amend Or Revoke the Order of August 22, 2014, Under CCP Sec. 473(B) and Mistake Inadvertence, Surprise Excusable Neglect, and to Hold the Hearing on Breach of the Settlement Agreement By the Defendants Laub is denied. The motion is untimely under CCP 1008 and the court lacks the jurisdiction to entertain the motion because judgment was entered on July 29, 2014. See Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

Motion To Declare Vasu A. Arora A Vexatious Litigant
Off Calendar Jan-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff VASU D. ARORA, Tel No. (408) 365-1277 appearing in pro per;
Atty. MARK R. MEYER of Losch & Ehrlich, Tel No. (415) 956-8400 appearing telephonically for Defendants Arnold Laub, P.C. dba Law Offices of Arnold Laub.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: DEFENDANT ARNOLD LAUB, PC DBA LAW OFFICES OF ARNOLD LAUB'S Motion To Declare Vasu A. Arora A Vexatious Litigant is granted under CCP 391(b)(2). The Court finds that Mr. Arora qualifies as a vexatious litigant under that section. However, the Court does not find the requirements have been met under CCP 391(b)(3). Mr. Arora shall be prohibited from filing any litigation against Arnold Laub dba Law Office of Arnold Laub or the attorneys of that office without first obtaining leave of the presiding judge. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

Off Calendar Oct-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Basu D. Arora's Motion To Amend Or Revoke The Order Of August 22, 2014, Under CCP 473; Mistake Inadvertence, Surprise, Excusable Neglect, And To Hold The Hearing On Breach Of The Settlement Agreement is Off calendar. The proof of service states that the motion was served by email. Rule of Court 2.251(b)(1)(A) requires a showing of consent to accept electronic service. No showing has been made that Defendants have agreed to electronic service. No opposition was filed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Order Of 9/29/14, Being A Fraud On The Court By The Defts Who Thereby Lacked Jurisidction; And Because Of Mistake, Inadvertence, Surprise And Excusable Neglect Under Ccp 473(B)
Off Calendar Aug-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - VASU D. ARORA, TEL. NO. 365-1277, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JOSEPH EHRLICH OF LOSCH & EHRLICH, TEL. NO. 956-8400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF VASU ARORA's Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Order Of 9/29/14, Being A Fraud On The Court By The Defendants Who Thereby Lacked Jurisidction; And Because Of Mistake, Inadvertence, Surprise And Excusable Neglect Under Ccp 473(B) is Denied. The motion is untimely under CCP 1005(b). The motion was filed and served on August 4. Moving party only provided 14 court days notice and did not account for the five additional calendar days required for mailing. Additionally, the motion is denied on the merits. Plaintiff does not provide an explanation as to why he did not file an opposition to the motion to dismiss or attend the July 29, 2014 hearing. The proofs of service filed by Defendants establish that Plaintiff was notified about the July 29, 2014 hearing date via email and mail. (Mark R. Meyer Declaration, Ex. A.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-24-2014 Continued To Apr-30-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Joseph Ehrlich, Esq. of Losch & Ehrlich, Tel. 415.956.8400, for defendants and moving party;
Vasu D. Arora, Tel. 408.365.1277, plaintiff and opposing party appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conlusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs LAW OFFICES OF ARNOLD LAUB and 807 MONTGOMERY STREET ASSOCIATES LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained. (CCP sec. 430.10(e) and (f).) The Court takes judicial notice of the First Amended Complaint filed in SF Superior Court case CGC-10-501595. As to the first cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claims involve the same primary right, inadequate payment of wages and insufficient meal and rest breaks, raised in previous litigation against Defendant (SF Superior Court case CGC-10-501595), and therefore are barred by res judicata. Alternatively, Plaintiff's claims are barred by the Settlement Agreement entered into on January 6, 2012, in which Plaintiff agreed to release Defendant from "any claims arising out of Employee's employment relationship with the Company." (First Amended Complaint ("FAC"), Ex. 5, Settlement Agreement, par. 11.) As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to clearly allege, if Plaintiff can in good faith, how Defendant breached the Settlement Agreement. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 11, 2014 at 4:00 pm, or the demurrer will be sustained and motion to strike will be granted.
